title: xyris mexiae
intro: Xyris mexiae, also known as the yellow-eyed grass, is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Xyridaceae. It is native to Mexico and is known for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, thanks to its expectorant and bronchodilatory properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Xyris mexiae is often prepared as an infusion or decoction, and it can also be used as a topical application to soothe skin irritations and wounds.
